news.title: Yelp’s updated AI assistant can answer questions and book a restaurant or service in one conversation
news.summary: Yelp said that users can ask questions about a restaurant and place an order or book a table in one conversation

news.title: Blue Energy raises $380M to build grid-scale nuclear reactors in shipyards
news.summary: By building reactors in shipyards, Blue Energy says it will tame nuclear power's cost problems, giving it access to cheaper financing.

news.title: Who is John Ternus, the incoming Apple CEO?
news.summary: Starting on September 1, Ternus will lead one of the world's most valuable companies, but if you're not a dedicated Apple enthusiast, you've probably never heard of this man, who has largely remained out of the spotlight until now.

news.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
news.summary: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

news.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
news.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of these countries except Japan.

news.title: The SpaceX IPO is a trillion-dollar gamble on the future of space
news.summary: The great SpaceX IPO is looming, allowing outside investors - including regular Joe Schmoes, or retail investors - to buy a stake in one of the buzziest and most controversial companies on the planet for the first time. news.title: Apple will have a product guy as CEO again
news.summary: John Ternus is going to be Apple's next CEO. And while outgoing CEO Tim Cook was lauded for his approach to logistics, Ternus' history is that of a product person. news.title: John Ternus will replace Tim Cook as Apple CEO
news.summary: Cook will be executive chairman, but will no longer run the company day to day.

news.title: They Built a Legendary Privacy Tool. Now They’re Sworn Enemies
